column1: Top 20 organic results currently ranking for a keyword. SERP analysis.

column5: Why it matters
Standard Semrush call parameters: auth, endpoint, target keyword, and country database.
Paging — limit = how many rows per call, offset = skip this many to get later pages.
Why it matters
Position — where this URL ranks in the SERP for the keyword (1 = top of page one).
Who is ranking and on which page?
Title — the meta title of the ranking page. Useful for spotting what's working in SERP.

column1: Bulk KD scoring. Up to 100 phrases per call.

column5: Why it matters
Standard auth + endpoint + country database parameters.
Which keywords to score in bulk?
Trim the response to specific columns.
Why it matters
How hard is each keyword to rank for?
Why it matters
Auth key, endpoint, the target URL/domain to analyse, and whether the target is a domain, subdomain, or single URL.
Why it matters
Authority Score (0-100) — Semrush's composite domain authority rating. Higher = stronger backlink profile.
Total number of inbound links pointing to the target — raw link volume.
Number of unique referring domains — more important than total links (10 links from 10 sites > 100 links from 1 site).
Number of unique pages linking to the target.
Number of unique IPs hosting linking sites — a diversity signal (10 sites on 10 IPs is healthier than 10 sites on 1 IP).
Number of unique class-C IP subnets — even stronger diversity signal. Concentration here often indicates link networks.
How many links pass authority?
Paid/sponsored vs user-generated link counts
What kinds of links predominate?
